options_zh: {"A": "对所有销售点终端使用共享 PIN，以使其更易于使用。", "B": "使用OAuth 允许每个用户登录云。", "C": "为每个用户分配他们所配发的 iPad 的唯一 PIN。", "D": "使用 Active Directory 和用户帐户通过 AD 用户 ID 和密码登录 iPad。"}
explanation_en: Using an enterprise authentication system like Active Directory that requires individuals to log in with their credentials provides the ability to determine who was logged in if a problem occurs and also allows Greg to quickly and easily remove users who are terminated or switch roles. Using a shared PIN provides no accountability, while unique PINs per user on specifically issued iPads mean that others will not be able to log in. OAuth alone does not provide the services and features Greg needs—it is an authorization service, not an authentication service.
explanation_zh: 使用 Active Directory 等要求个人使用凭据登录的企业身份验证系统，可以在出现问题时确定谁登录，并且还允许 Greg 快速轻松地删除被终止的用户或切换角色。使用共享 PIN 不提供任何责任，而专门发行的 iPad 上每个用户的唯一 PIN 意味着其他人将无法登录。OAuth 本身并不能提供 Greg 所需的服务和功能 - 它是一种授权服务，而不是身份验证服务。

question_en: What is the best way to provide accountability for the use of identities?
question_zh: 为身份使用提供问责制的最佳方式是什么？
options_zh: {"A": "日志记录", "B": "授权", "C": "数字签名", "D": "1 类身份验证"}
explanation_en: Logging systems can provide accountability for identity systems by tracking the actions, changes, and other activities a user or account performs.
explanation_zh: 日志系统可以通过跟踪用户或帐户执行的操作、更改和其他活动来为身份系统提供责任。

question_en: Jim has worked in human relations, payroll, and customer service roles in his company over the past few years. What type of process should his company perform to ensure that he has appropriate rights?
question_zh: 过去几年，吉姆在公司担任过人际关系、薪资和客户服务职务。他的公司应该执行什么类型的流程来确保他拥有适当的权利？
options_zh: {"A": "重新配置", "B": "账户审查", "C": "权限蔓延", "D": "账户撤销"}
explanation_en: As an employee’s role changes, they often experience privilege creep, which is the accumulation of old rights and roles. Account review is the process of reviewing accounts and ensuring that their rights match their owners’ role and job requirements. Account revocation removes accounts, while re-provisioning might occur if an employee was terminated and returned or took a leave of absence and returned.
explanation_zh: 随着员工角色的转变，他们经常会经历权限蔓延，这是旧权限和角色的积累。帐户审查是审查帐户并确保其权利与其所有者的角色和工作要求相匹配的过程。帐户撤销会删除帐户，而如果员工被解雇并返回或请假并返回，则可能会发生重新配置。

question_en: Biba is what type of access control model?
question_zh: Biba 是什么类型的访问控制模型？
options_zh: {"A": "MAC", "B": "DAC", "C": "角色 BAC", "D": "ABAC"}
explanation_en: Biba uses a lattice to control access and is a form of the mandatory access control (MAC) model. It does not use rules, roles, or attributes, nor does it allow user discretion. Users can create content at their level or lower but cannot decide who gets access, levels are not roles, and attributes are not used to make decisions on access control.
explanation_zh: Biba 使用网格来控制访问，是强制访问控制 (MAC) 模型的一种形式。它不使用规则、角色或属性，也不允许用户自行决定。用户可以在其级别或更低级别创建内容，但无法决定谁有权访问，级别不是角色，并且属性不用于做出访问控制决策。

question_en: Which of the following is a client/server protocol designed to allow network access servers to authenticate remote users by sending access request messages to a central server?
question_zh: 以下哪一项是客户端/服务器协议，旨在允许网络访问服务器通过向中央服务器发送访问请求消息来对远程用户进行身份验证？
options_zh: {"A": "Kerberos", "B": "EAP", "C": "RADIUS", "D": "OAuth"}
explanation_en: RADIUS is an AAA protocol used to provide authentication and authorization; it’s often used for modems, wireless networks, and network devices. It uses network access servers to send access requests to central RADIUS servers. Kerberos is a ticket-based authentication protocol; OAuth is an open standard for authentication allowing the use of credentials from one site on third-party sites; and EAP is the Extensible Authentication Protocol, an authentication framework often used for wireless networks.
explanation_zh: RADIUS 是用于提供身份验证和授权的 AAA 协议；它通常用于调制解调器、无线网络和网络设备。它使用网络访问服务器向中央 RADIUS 服务器发送访问请求。 Kerberos 是一种基于票证的身份验证协议； OAuth 是一种开放的身份验证标准，允许在第三方站点上使用来自一个站点的凭据； EAP是可扩展身份验证协议，是一种常用于无线网络的身份验证框架。

question_en: Henry is working with a web application development team on their authentication and authorization process for his company’s new application. The team wants to make session IDs as secure as possible. Which of the following is not a best practice that Henry should recommend?
options_en: {"A": "The session ID token should be predictable.", "B": "The session ID should have at least 64 bits of entropy.", "C": "The session length should be at least 128 bits.", "D": "The session ID should be meaningless."}
question_zh: Henry 正在与 Web 应用程序开发团队合作，为其公司的新应用程序进行身份验证和授权流程。该团队希望使会话 ID 尽可能安全。以下哪项不是 Henry 应该推荐的最佳实践？
options_zh: {"A": "会话 ID 令牌应该是可预测的。", "B": "会话 ID 应该至少有 64 位熵。", "C": "会话长度至少应为 128 位。", "D": "会话ID应该没有意义。"}
explanation_en: Web application development best practices currently recommend the use of long session IDs (128 bits or longer) that have sufficient entropy (randomness) to ensure that they will not be easily duplicated or brute forced. It is also a best practice to make sure the session ID itself is meaningless to prevent information disclosure attacks. Session IDs should expire, however, because a session that never expires could eventually be brute forced even if all of these recommendations were met.
explanation_zh: Web 应用程序开发最佳实践目前建议使用具有足够熵（随机性）的长会话 ID（128 位或更长），以确保它们不会被轻易复制或暴力破解。确保会话 ID 本身没有意义也是防止信息泄露攻击的最佳实践。然而，会话 ID 应该过期，因为即使满足所有这些建议，永不过期的会话最终也可能会被暴力破解。

question_en: Angela uses a sniffer to monitor traffic from a RADIUS server configured with default settings. What protocol should she monitor, and what traffic will she be able to read?
options_en: {"A": "UDP, none. All RADIUS traffic is encrypted.", "B": "TCP, all traffic but the passwords, which are encrypted.", "C": "UDP, all traffic but the passwords, which are encrypted.", "D": "TCP, none. All RADIUS traffic is encrypted."}
question_zh: Angela 使用嗅探器监控来自配置了默认设置的 RADIUS 服务器的流量。她应该监控什么协议，以及她能够读取哪些流量？
options_zh: {"A": "UDP，无。所有 RADIUS 流量均已加密。", "B": "TCP，除密码外的所有流量均已加密。", "C": "UDP，除密码外的所有流量均已加密。", "D": "TCP，无。所有 RADIUS 流量均已加密。"}
explanation_en: By default, RADIUS uses UDP and only encrypts passwords. RADIUS supports TCP and TLS, but this is not a default setting.
explanation_zh: 默认情况下，RADIUS 使用 UDP，仅对密码进行加密。 RADIUS 支持 TCP 和 TLS，但这不是默认设置。

question_en: What type of access control best describes NAC’s posture assessment capability?
options_en: {"A": "A mandatory access control", "B": "A risk-based access control", "C": "A discretionary access control", "D": "A role-based access control"}
question_zh: 哪种类型的访问控制最能描述 NAC 的姿势评估能力？
options_zh: {"A": "强制访问控制", "B": "基于风险的访问控制", "C": "自主访问控制", "D": "基于角色的访问控制"}
explanation_en: NAC’s posturing capability determines if a system is sufficiently secure and compliant enough to connect to a network. This is a form of risk-based access control, as systems that are not compliant are considered higher risk and either are placed in a quarantine and remediation network or zone or are prohibited from connecting to the network until they are compliant.
explanation_zh: NAC 的姿势能力决定系统是否足够安全和​​合规，足以连接到网络。这是基于风险的访问控制的一种形式，因为不合规的系统被认为风险较高，并且要么被放置在隔离和补救网络或区域中，要么被禁止连接到网络，直到它们合规为止。

question_en: When an application or system allows a logged-in user to perform specific actions, it is an example of what?
question_zh: 当应用程序或系统允许登录用户执行特定操作时，这是一个什么示例？
explanation_en: Authorization provides a user with capabilities or rights. Roles and group management are both methods that could be used to match users with rights. Logins are used to validate a user.
explanation_zh: 授权为用户提供能力或权限。角色和组管理都是可以用来将用户与权限进行匹配的方法。登录用于验证用户。

question_en: Alex has been employed by his company for more than a decade and has held a number of positions in the company. During an audit, it is discovered that he has access to shared folders and applications because of his former roles. What issue has Alex’s company encountered?
question_zh: Alex 在他的公司工作了十多年，并在公司担任过多个职位。在审核过程中，发现他由于以前的角色而有权访问共享文件夹和应用程序。 Alex的公司遇到了什么问题？
options_zh: {"A": "过度配置", "B": "未经授权的访问", "C": "权限蔓延", "D": "帐户审核"}
explanation_en: Privilege creep occurs when users retain from roles they held previously rights they do not need to accomplish their current job. Unauthorized access occurs when an unauthorized user accesses files. Excessive provisioning is not a term used to describe permissions issues, and account review would help find issues like this.
explanation_zh: 当用户保留之前拥有的角色但完成当前工作不需要的权限时，就会发生权限蔓延。当未经授权的用户访问文件时，就会发生未经授权的访问。过度配置并不是用于描述权限问题的术语，帐户审查将有助于发现此类问题。

question_en: Geoff wants to prevent privilege escalation attacks in his organization. Which of the following practices is most likely to prevent horizontal privilege escalation?
options_en: {"A": "Multifactor authentication", "B": "Limiting permissions for groups and accounts", "C": "Disabling unused ports and services", "D": "Sanitizing user inputs to applications"}
question_zh: Geoff 希望防止其组织中出现权限升级攻击。以下哪种做法最有可能防止横向特权升级？
options_zh: {"A": "多重身份验证", "B": "限制组和帐户的权限", "C": "禁用未使用的端口和服务", "D": "清理应用程序的用户输入"}
explanation_en: Multifactor authentication is most likely to limit horizontal privilege escalation by making it difficult to access user accounts and to authenticate to a compromised account. Limiting permissions for groups and accounts can also help, but disabling unused ports and services and sanitizing user inputs both address threats that are most frequently associated with vertical privilege escalation attacks.
explanation_zh: 多重身份验证最有可能通过使访问用户帐户和对受损帐户进行身份验证变得困难来限制水平权限升级。限制组和帐户的权限也有帮助，但禁用未使用的端口和服务以及清理用户输入都可以解决最常与垂直权限升级攻击相关的威胁。

question_en: Jim’s Microsoft Exchange environment includes servers that are located in local data centers at multiple business offices around the world as well as an Office 365 deployment for employees who are not located at one of those offices. Identities are created and used in both environments and will work in both. What type of federated system is Jim running?
options_en: {"A": "A primary cloud system", "B": "A primary on-premise system", "C": "A hybrid system", "D": "A multitenant system"}
question_zh: Jim 的 Microsoft Exchange 环境包括位于全球多个业务办事处的本地数据中心的服务器，以及为不在这些办事处之一的员工部署的 Office 365。身份在这两种环境中创建和使用，并且在这两种环境中都有效。 Jim 正在运行什么类型的联合系统？
options_zh: {"A": "主要云系统", "B": "主要本地系统", "C": "混合系统", "D": "多租户系统"}
explanation_en: Hybrid systems use both on-premises and cloud identity and services to provide resources and tools in both environments. While they can be complex, hybrid systems also provide a migration path to a fully cloud deployment or for a fault tolerant design that can handle on-premises or cloud outages while remaining functional.
explanation_zh: 混合系统同时使用本地和云身份和服务，在两种环境中提供资源和工具。虽然它们可能很复杂，但混合系统还提供了到完全云部署或容错设计的迁移路径，可以在保持功能的同时处理本地或云中断。

question_en: What type of access control scheme is shown in the following table? Highly Sensitive Red Blue Green Confidential Purple Orange Yellow Internal Use Black Gray White Public Clear Clear Clear
question_zh: 下表所示是什么类型的访问控制方案？高灵敏度 红色 蓝色 绿色 机密 紫色 橙色 黄色 内部使用 黑色 灰色 白色 公共 透明 透明 透明
options_zh: {"A": "RBAC", "B": "DAC", "C": "MAC", "D": "TBAC"}
explanation_en: Mandatory access controls use a lattice or matrix to describe how classification labels relate to each other. In this image, classification levels are set for each of the labels shown. A discretionary access control (DAC) system would show how the owner of the objects allows access. RBAC could be either ruleor role-based access control and would use either system-wide rules or roles. Task-based access control (TBAC) would list tasks for users.
explanation_zh: 强制访问控制使用点阵或矩阵来描述分类标签如何相互关联。在此图像中，为显示的每个标签设置了分类级别。自主访问控制 (DAC) 系统将显示对象所有者如何允许访问。 RBAC 可以是基于规则或基于角色的访问控制，并且可以使用系统范围的规则或角色。基于任务的访问控制（TBAC）将为用户列出任务。

question_en: Michelle’s company is creating a new division by splitting the marketing and communications departments into two separate groups. She wants to create roles that provide access to resources used by each group. What should she do to maintain the appropriate security and rights for each group?
options_en: {"A": "Put both the marketing and communications teams into the existing group because they will have similar access requirements.", "B": "Keep the marketing team in the existing group and create a new communications group based on their specific needs.", "C": "Keep the communications team in the existing group and create a new marketing group based on their specific needs.", "D": "Create two new groups, assess which rights they need to perform their roles, and then add additional rights if required."}
question_zh: 米歇尔的公司正在创建一个新部门，将营销和传播部门分为两个独立的组。她想要创建提供对每个组使用的资源的访问权限的角色。她应该怎样做才能维护每个群体的适当安全和权利？
options_zh: {"A": "将营销和传播团队都放入现有组中，因为他们有类似的访问要求。", "B": "将营销团队保留在现有团队中，并根据他们的具体需求创建新的传播团队。", "C": "将传播团队保留在现有团队中，并根据他们的具体需求创建新的营销团队。", "D": "创建两个新组，评估他们执行其角色所需的权限，然后根据需要添加其他权限。"}
explanation_en: Copying existing rights to new groups that have different needs will often result in overly broad privileges. Michelle should create new groups, move all staff into the appropriate groups, and then ensure that they have the access and permissions they need.
explanation_zh: 将现有权限复制给具有不同需求的新组通常会导致权限过于广泛。米歇尔应该创建新组，将所有员工移至适当的组，然后确保他们拥有所需的访问权限和权限。

question_en: When a subject claims an identity, what process is occurring?
question_zh: 当主体声明身份时，正在发生什么过程？
explanation_en: The process of a subject claiming or professing an identity is known as identification. Authorization verifies the identity of a subject by checking a factor like a password. Logins typically include both identification and authorization, and token presentation is a type of authentication.
explanation_zh: 主体声明或表明身份的过程称为身份识别。授权通过检查密码等因素来验证主体的身份。登录通常包括身份验证和授权，令牌呈现是一种身份验证。

question_en: Dogs, guards, and fences are all common examples of what type of control?
question_zh: 狗、警卫和栅栏都是哪种控制类型的常见示例？
explanation_en: Dogs, guards, and fences are all examples of physical controls. While dogs and guards might detect a problem, fences cannot, so they are not all examples of detective controls. None of these controls would help repair or restore functionality after an issue, and thus they are not recovery controls, nor are they administrative controls that involve policy or procedures, although the guards might refer to them when performing their duties.
explanation_zh: 狗、警卫和栅栏都是物理控制的例子。虽然狗和警卫可能会发现问题，但栅栏却不能，因此它们并不都是侦探控制的例子。这些控制措施都无法帮助修复或恢复问题后的功能，因此它们不是恢复控制措施，也不是涉及策略或程序的管理控制措施，尽管警卫在履行职责时可能会参考它们。

question_en: Susan’s organization is updating its password policy and wants to use the strongest possible passwords. What password requirement will have the highest impact in preventing brute-force attacks?
options_en: {"A": "Change maximum age from 1 year to 180 days.", "B": "Increase the minimum password length from 8 characters to 16 characters.", "C": "Increase the password complexity so that at least three character classes (such as uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols) are required.", "D": "Retain a password history of at least four passwords to prevent reuse."}
question_zh: Susan 的组织正在更新其密码策略，并希望使用最强的密码。什么密码要求对防止暴力攻击影响最大？
options_zh: {"A": "将最大年龄从 1 岁更改为 180 天。", "B": "将最小密码长度从 8 个字符增加到 16 个字符。", "C": "增加密码复杂度，至少需要三种字符类别（例如大写、小写、数字和符号）。", "D": "保留至少四个密码的密码历史记录，以防止重复使用。"}
explanation_en: Password complexity is driven by length, and a longer password will be more effective against brute-force attacks than a shorter password. Each character of additional length increases the difficulty by the size of the potential character set (for example, a single lowercase character makes the passwords 26 times more difficult to crack). While each of the other settings is useful for a strong password policy, they won’t have the same impact on brute-force attacks.
explanation_zh: 密码复杂性由长度决定，较长的密码比较短的密码更能有效抵御暴力攻击。每个额外长度的字符都会增加潜在字符集大小的难度（例如，单个小写字符使密码的破解难度增加 26 倍）。虽然其他每个设置对于强密码策略都很有用，但它们不会对暴力攻击产生相同的影响。

question_en: Alaina is performing a regularly scheduled review for service accounts. Which of the following events should she be most concerned about?
options_en: {"A": "An interactive login for the service account", "B": "A password change for the service account", "C": "Limitations placed on the service account’s rights", "D": "Local use of the service account"}
question_zh: Alaina 正在对服务帐户进行定期审核。她最应该关注以下哪项事件？
options_zh: {"A": "服务帐户的交互式登录", "B": "服务帐户的密码更改", "C": "对服务帐户权限的限制", "D": "服务帐户的本地使用"}
explanation_en: Interactive login for a service account is a critical warning sign, either of compromise or bad administrative practices. In either case, Alaina should immediately work to determine why the account logged in, what occurred, and if the interactive login was done remotely or locally. A remote interactive login for a service account in any professionally maintained environment is an almost guaranteed sign of compromise. Password changes for service accounts may be done as part of ongoing password expiration processes, limitations should always be placed on service accounts rights to ensure that they are only those required, and a local use of the service account as part of the service is a normal event.
explanation_zh: 服务帐户的交互式登录是一个严重的警告信号，无论是妥协还是不良的管理实践。无论哪种情况，Alaina 都应立即确定帐户登录的原因、发生了什么以及交互式登录是远程还是本地完成。在任何专业维护的环境中，服务帐户的远程交互式登录几乎都是妥协的迹象。服务帐户的密码更改可以作为正在进行的密码过期过程的一部分来完成，应始终对服务帐户权限进行限制，以确保它们只是所需的权限，并且在本地使用服务帐户作为服务的一部分是正常事件。

question_en: When might an organization using biometrics choose to allow a higher FRR instead of a higher FAR?
options_en: {"A": "When security is more important than usability", "B": "When false rejection is not a concern due to data quality", "C": "When the CER of the system is not known", "D": "When the CER of the system is very high"}
question_zh: 使用生物识别技术的组织何时可能选择允许更高的 FRR 而不是更高的 FAR？
options_zh: {"A": "当安全性比可用性更重要时", "B": "当由于数据质量而无需担心错误拒绝时", "C": "当系统的 CER 未知时", "D": "当系统的 CER 非常高时"}
explanation_en: Organizations that have very strict security requirements that don’t have a tolerance for false acceptance want to lower the false acceptance rate, or FAR, to be as near to zero as possible. That often means that the false rejection rate, or FRR, increases. Different biometric technologies or a better registration method can help improve biometric performance, but false rejections due to data quality are not typically a concern with modern biometric systems. In this case, knowing the crossover error rate, or CER, or having a very high CER doesn’t help the decision.
explanation_zh: 具有非常严格的安全要求且不能容忍错误接受的组织希望将错误接受率 (FAR) 降低到接近于零可能的。这通常意味着错误拒绝率 (FRR) 会增加。不同的生物识别技术或更好的注册方法可以帮助提高生物识别性能，但由于数据质量导致的错误拒绝通常不是现代生物识别系统的问题。在这种情况下，了解交叉错误率 (CER) 或具有非常高的 CER 并不能帮助做出决定。

question_en: After recent reports of undesired access to workstations after hours, Derek has been asked to find a way to ensure that maintenance staff cannot log in to workstations in business offices. The maintenance staff members do have systems in their break rooms and their offices for the organization, which they still need access to. What should Derek do to meet this need?
options_en: {"A": "Require multifactor authentication and only allow office staff to have multifactor tokens.", "B": "Use rule-based access control to prevent logins after hours in the business area.", "C": "Use role-based access control by setting up a group that contains all maintenance staff and then give that group rights to log into only the designated workstations.", "D": "Use geofencing to only allow logins in maintenance areas."}
question_zh: 最近收到有关下班后工作站被非法访问的报告后，Derek 被要求找到一种方法来确保维护人员无法登录业务办公室的工作站。维护人员在其休息室和组织办公室中确实拥有系统，但他们仍然需要访问这些系统。 Derek 应该做什么来满足这个需求呢？
options_zh: {"A": "需要多重身份验证，并且只允许办公室工作人员拥有多重令牌。", "B": "使用基于规则的访问控制来防止营业区域下班后登录。", "C": "使用基于角色的访问控制，设置一个包含所有维护人员的组，然后授予该组仅登录指定工作站的权限。", "D": "使用地理围栏仅允许在维护区域登录。"}
explanation_en: The most efficient use of Derek’s time would be to create a group that is populated with all maintenance staff and then to give that group login rights only to the designated PCs. While time-based constraints might help, in this case, it would continue to allow maintenance staff to log in to PCs they are not intended for use during business hours, leaving a gap in the control. Multifactor authentication as described does not meet the requirements of the scenario but may be a good idea overall for greater security for authentication across the organization. Geofencing is typically not accurate enough to rely on inside of buildings for specific PCs.
explanation_zh: 最有效利用 Derek 时间的方法是创建一个包含所有维护人员的组，然后只将该组登录权限授予指定的 PC。虽然基于时间的限制可能会有所帮助，但在这种情况下，它将继续允许维护人员登录到他们不打算在工作时间使用的电脑，从而在控制中留下空白。所描述的多因素身份验证不满足场景的要求，但总体而言对于提高整个组织的身份验证安全性可能是一个好主意。地理围栏通常不够准确，无法依赖建筑物内部的特定 PC。

question_en: Nick wants to do session management for his web application. Which of the following are common web application session management techniques or methods? (Select all that apply.)
options_en: {"A": "IP tracking", "B": "Cookies", "C": "URL rewriting", "D": "TLS tokens For questions 43–45, please use your knowledge of SAML integrations and security architecture design and refer to the following scenario and diagram: Alex is in charge of SAML integration with a major third-party partner that provides a variety of business productivity services for his organization. Home User SAML Organization Integrated Identity 1. User Third-Party Provider attempts Service to access application 2. Redirect 3. Browser to SSO accesses URL SSO URL 4. Home 5. SAML organization response authenticates 6. Browser sent to user and sends browser Web sends SAML SAML Browser response response to third party 7. SAML response verified, user logged into third-party application"}
question_zh: Nick 希望为其 Web 应用程序进行会话管理。以下哪些是常见的 Web 应用程序会话管理技术或方法？ （选择所有适用项。）
options_zh: {"A": "IP 跟踪", "B": "Cookie", "C": "URL 重写", "D": "TLS 令牌 对于问题 43-45，请利用您对 SAML 集成和安全架构设计的了解，并参考以下场景和图表： Alex 负责与一家为其组织提供各种业务生产力服务的主要第三方合作伙伴进行 SAML 集成。 Home 用户 SAML 组织集成身份 1. 用户第三方提供商尝试服务访问应用程序 2. 重定向 3. 浏览器到 SSO 访问 URL SSO URL 4. Home 5. SAML 组织响应进行身份验证 6. 浏览器发送给用户并发送浏览器 Web 将 SAML 响应发送到第三方 7. SAML 响应经过验证，用户登录到第三方应用程序"}
explanation_en: Common session management techniques include the use of cookies, hidden form fields, URL rewriting, and built-in frameworks like Java’s HTTPSession. IP tracking may be included in session information but is not itself a complete session identifier, and TLS token binding is used to make TLS sessions more secure, not to provide session identification.
explanation_zh: 常见会话管理技术包括使用 cookie、隐藏表单字段、URL 重写、以及 Java 的 HTTPSession 等内置框架。 IP 跟踪可能包含在会话信息中，但其本身并不是完整的会话标识符，并且 TLS 令牌绑定用于使 TLS 会话更加安全，而不是提供会话标识。

question_en: Alex is concerned about eavesdropping on the SAML traffic and also wants to ensure that forged assertions will not be successful. What should he do to prevent these potential attacks?
options_en: {"A": "Use SAML’s secure mode to provide secure authentication.", "B": "Implement TLS using a strong cipher suite, which will protect against both types of attacks.", "C": "Implement TLS using a strong cipher suite and use digital signatures.", "D": "Implement TLS using a strong cipher suite and message hashing."}
question_zh: Alex 担心 SAML 流量被窃听，并且还希望确保伪造断言不会成功。他应该做什么来防止这些潜在的攻击？
options_zh: {"A": "使用SAML的安全模式提供安全认证。", "B": "使用强大的密码套件实施 TLS，这将防止这两种类型的攻击。", "C": "使用强大的密码套件实现 TLS 并使用数字签名。", "D": "使用强大的密码套件和消息哈希实现 TLS。"}
explanation_en: TLS provides message confidentiality and integrity, which can prevent eavesdropping. When paired with digital signatures, which provide integrity and authentication, forged assertions can also be defeated. SAML does not have a security mode and relies on TLS and digital signatures to ensure security if needed. Message hashing without a signature would help prevent modification of the message but won’t necessarily provide authentication.
explanation_zh: TLS 提供消息机密性和完整性，可以防止窃听。当与提供完整性和身份验证的数字签名配合使用时，伪造的断言也可以被击败。 SAML 没有安全模式，需要时依靠 TLS 和数字签名来确保安全。没有签名的消息散列将有助于防止消息被修改，但不一定提供身份验证。

question_en: If Alex’s organization is one that is primarily made up of off-site, traveling users, what availability risk does integration of critical business applications to on-site authentication create, and how could he solve it?
options_en: {"A": "Third-party integration may not be trustworthy; use SSL and digital signatures.", "B": "If the home organization is offline, traveling users won’t be able to access third-party applications; implement a hybrid cloud/local authentication system.", "C": "Local users may not be properly redirected to the third-party services; implement a local gateway.", "D": "Browsers may not properly redirect; use host files to ensure that issues with redirects are resolved."}
question_zh: 如果 Alex 的组织主要由异地、出差的用户组成，那么关键业务应用程序与现场身份验证的集成会产生哪些可用性风险，他该如何解决呢？
options_zh: {"A": "第三方集成可能不值得信赖；使用 SSL 和数字签名。", "B": "如果本地组织离线，出差用户将无法访问第三方应用程序；实施混合云/本地身份验证系统。", "C": "本地用户可能无法正确重定向到第三方服务；实施本地网关。", "D": "浏览器可能无法正确重定向；使用主机文件确保重定向问题得到解决。"}
explanation_en: Integration with cloud-based third parties that rely on local authentication can fail if the local organization’s internet connectivity or servers are offline. Adopting a hybrid cloud and local authentication system can ensure that internet or server outages are handled, allowing authentication to work regardless of where the user is or if their home organization is online. Using encrypted and signed communication does not address availability, redirects are a configuration issue with the third party, and a local gateway won’t handle remote users. Also, host files don’t help with availability issues with services other than DNS.
explanation_zh: 如果本地组织的互联网连接或服务器离线，与依赖本地身份验证的基于云的第三方集成可能会失败。采用混合云和本地身份验证系统可以确保处理互联网或服务器中断，无论用户身在何处或其家庭组织是否在线，身份验证都可以正常工作。使用加密和签名的通信并不能解决可用性问题，重定向是第三方的配置问题，并且本地网关无法处理远程用户。此外，主机文件无法帮助解决 DNS 以外的服务的可用性问题。

question_en: What solution can best help address concerns about third parties that control SSO redirects as shown in step 2 in the diagram?
options_en: {"A": "An awareness campaign about trusted third parties", "B": "TLS", "C": "Handling redirects at the local site", "D": "Implementing an IPS to capture SSO redirect attacks"}
question_zh: 什么解决方案最能帮助解决有关控制 SSO 重定向的第三方的担忧（如图中步骤 2 所示）？
options_zh: {"A": "关于受信任第三方的意识活动", "B": "TLS", "C": "在本地站点处理重定向", "D": "实施 IPS 来捕获 SSO 重定向攻击"}
explanation_en: While many solutions are technical, if a trusted third party redirects to an unexpected authentication site, awareness is often the best defense. Using TLS would keep the transaction confidential but would not prevent the redirect. Handling redirects locally works only for locally hosted sites, and using a third-party service requires off-site redirects. An IPS might detect an attacker’s redirect, but tracking the multitude of load-balanced servers most large providers use can be challenging, if not impossible. In addition, an IPS relies on visibility into the traffic, and SAML integrations should be encrypted for security, which would require a man-in-the-middle type of IPS to be configured.
explanation_zh: 虽然许多解决方案都是技术性的，但如果受信任的第三方重定向到意外的身份验证站点，意识通常是最好的防御措施。使用 TLS 可以保证交易的机密性，但不会阻止重定向。在本地处理重定向仅适用于本地托管的站点，使用第三方服务需要场外重定向。 IPS 可能会检测到攻击者的重定向，但跟踪大多数大型提供商使用的众多负载平衡服务器即使不是不可能，也可能具有挑战性。此外，IPS 依赖于流量的可见性，并且为了安全起见，应该对 SAML 集成进行加密，这需要配置中间人类型的 IPS。

question_en: Susan has been asked to recommend whether her organization should use a MAC scheme or a DAC scheme. If flexibility and scalability are important requirements for implementing access controls, which scheme should she recommend and why?
options_en: {"A": "MAC, because it provides greater scalability and flexibility because you can simply add more labels as needed", "B": "DAC, because allowing individual administrators to make choices about the objects they control provides scalability and flexibility", "C": "MAC, because compartmentalization is well suited to flexibility and adding compartments will allow it to scale well", "D": "DAC, because a central decision process allows quick responses and will provide scalability by reducing the number of decisions required and flexibility by moving those decisions to a central authority"}
question_zh: Susan 被要求建议她的组织是否应该使用 MAC 方案或 DAC 方案。如果灵活性和可扩展性是实施访问控制的重要要求，她应该推荐哪种方案？为什么？
options_zh: {"A": "MAC，因为它提供了更大的可扩展性和灵活性，因为您可以根据需要添加更多标签", "B": "DAC，因为允许各个管理员对其控制的对象进行选择提供了可扩展性和灵活性", "C": "MAC，因为分区非常适合灵活性，添加分区将使其能够很好地扩展", "D": "DAC，因为中央决策过程允许快速响应，并将通过减少所需的决策数量和通过移动来提供灵活性将这些决策交给中央机构"}
explanation_en: Discretionary access control (DAC) can provide greater scalability by leveraging many administrators, and those administrators can add flexibility by making decisions about access to their objects without fitting into an inflexible mandatory access control system (MAC). MAC is more secure due to the strong set of controls it provides, but it does not scale as well as DAC and is relatively inflexible in comparison.
explanation_zh: 自主访问控制 (DAC) 可以通过利用许多管理员来提供更大的可扩展性，并且这些管理员可以通过做出有关访问其对象的决策来增加灵活性，而无需适应不灵活的强制访问控制系统 (MAC)。 MAC 由于提供了强大的控制集而更加安全，但它的扩展性不如 DAC，并且相比之下相对不灵活。

question_en: Which of the following tools is not typically used to verify that a provisioning process was followed in a way that ensures that the organization’s security policy is being followed?
options_en: {"A": "Log review", "B": "Manual review of permissions", "C": "Signature-based detection", "D": "Review the audit trail"}
question_zh: 以下哪种工具通常不用于验证是否遵循配置流程以确保遵循组织的安全策略？
options_zh: {"A": "日志审核", "B": "手动审核权限", "C": "基于签名的检测", "D": "审核审核跟踪"}
explanation_en: While signature-based detection is used to detect attacks, review of provisioning processes typically involves checking logs, reviewing the audit trail, or performing a manual review of permissions granted during the provisioning process.
explanation_zh: 虽然基于签名的检测用于检测攻击，但配置流程的审核通常涉及检查日志、审核审核跟踪或对配置过程中授予的权限进行手动审核。

question_en: Jessica needs to send information about services she is provisioning to a third-party organization. What standards-based markup language should she choose to build the interface?
question_zh: Jessica 需要将有关她正在配置的服务的信息发送给第三方组织。她应该选择哪种基于标准的标记语言来构建界面？
options_zh: {"A": "SAML", "B": "SOAP", "C": "SPML", "D": "XACML"}
explanation_en: Service Provisioning Markup Language, or SPML, is an XML-based language designed to allow platforms to generate and respond to provisioning requests. SAML is used to make authorization and authentication data, while XACML is used to describe access controls. SOAP, or Simple Object Access Protocol, is a messaging protocol and could be used for any XML messaging but is not a markup language itself.
explanation_zh: 服务配置标记语言 (SPML) 是一种基于 XML 的语言，旨在允许平台生成和响应配置请求。 SAML 用于制作授权和认证数据，而 XACML 用于描述访问控制。 SOAP（即简单对象访问协议）是一种消息传递协议，可用于任何 XML 消息传递，但本身并不是一种标记语言。

question_en: During a penetration test, Chris recovers a file containing hashed passwords for the system he is attempting to access. What type of attack is most likely to succeed against the hashed passwords?
options_en: {"A": "A brute-force attack", "B": "A pass-the-hash attack", "C": "A rainbow table attack", "D": "A salt recovery attack"}
question_zh: 在渗透测试期间，Chris 恢复了一个文件，其中包含他尝试访问的系统的哈希密码。针对哈希密码的哪种类型的攻击最有可能成功？
options_zh: {"A": "暴力攻击", "B": "哈希传递攻击", "C": "彩虹表攻击", "D": "盐恢复攻击"}
explanation_en: Rainbow tables are databases of pre-hashed passwords paired with high-speed lookup functions. Since they can quickly compare known hashes against those in a file, using rainbow tables is the fastest way to quickly determine passwords from hashes. A brute-force attack may eventually succeed but will be very slow against most hashes. Pass-the-hash attacks rely on sniffed or otherwise acquired NTLM or LanMan hashes being sent to a system to avoid the need to know a user’s password. Salts are data added to a hash to avoid the use of tools like rainbow tables. A salt added to a password means the hash won’t match a rainbow table generated without the same salt.
explanation_zh: 彩虹表是预哈希密码与高速查找功能配对的数据库。由于他们可以快速将已知的哈希值与文件中的哈希值进行比较，因此使用彩虹表是根据哈希值快速确定密码的最快方法。暴力攻击最终可能会成功，但对于大多数哈希来说，速度会非常慢。哈希传递攻击依赖于嗅探或以其他方式获取的 NTLM 或 LanMan 哈希发送到系统，以避免需要知道用户的密码。盐是添加到哈希中的数据，以避免使用彩虹表等工具。添加到密码中的盐意味着哈希将与没有相同盐生成的彩虹表不匹配。

question_en: Google’s identity integration with a variety of organizations and applications across domains is an example of which of the following?
question_zh: Google 与跨域的各种组织和应用程序的身份集成是以下哪项示例？
explanation_en: Google’s federation with other applications and organizations allows single sign-on as well as management of their electronic identity and its related attributes. While this is an example of SSO, it goes beyond simple single sign-on. Provisioning provides accounts and rights, and a public key infrastructure is used for certificate management.
explanation_zh: Google 与其他应用程序和组织的联合允许单点登录以及管理其电子身份及其相关属性。虽然这是 SSO 的示例，但它不仅仅是简单的单点登录。配置提供帐户和权限，公钥基础设施用于证书管理。

question_en: Amanda starts at her new job and finds that she has access to a variety of systems that she does not need to accomplish her job. What problem has she encountered?
question_zh: Amanda 开始她的新工作，发现她可以访问完成工作所需的各种系统。她遇到了什么问题？
options_zh: {"A": "权限蠕变", "B": "权限冲突", "C": "最小权限", "D": "过多权限"}
explanation_en: When users have more rights than they need to accomplish their job, they have excessive privileges. This is a violation of the concept of least privilege. Unlike creeping privileges, this is a provisioning or rights management issue rather than a problem of retention of rights the user needed but no longer requires. Rights collision is a made-up term and thus is not an issue here.
explanation_zh: 当用户拥有的权限多于完成其工作所需的权限时，他们就拥有过多的权限。这违反了最小特权的概念。与蠕变特权不同，这是一个配置或权限管理问题，而不是保留用户需要但不再需要的权限的问题。权利冲突是一个虚构的术语，因此不是这里的问题。

question_en: When Chris verifies an individual’s identity and adds a unique identifier like a user ID to an identity system, what process has occurred?
question_zh: 当 Chris 验证个人身份并将用户 ID 等唯一标识符添加到身份系统时，发生了哪些过程？
options_zh: {"A": "身份证明", "B": "注册", "C": "目录管理", "D": "会话管理"}
explanation_en: Registration is the process of adding a user to an identity management system. This includes creating their unique identifier and adding any attribute information that is associated with their identity. Proofing occurs when the user provides information to prove who they are. Directories are managed to maintain lists of users, services, and other items. Session management tracks application and user sessions.
explanation_zh: 注册是将用户添加到身份管理系统的过程。这包括创建其唯一标识符并添加与其身份关联的任何属性信息。当用户提供信息来证明他们是谁时，就会发生校对。目录的管理是为了维护用户、服务和其他项目的列表。会话管理跟踪应用程序和用户会话。

question_en: Selah wants to provide accountability for actions performed via her organization’s main line of business application. What controls are most frequently used to provide accountability in a situation like this? (Select all that apply.)
options_en: {"A": "Enable audit logging.", "B": "Provide every staff member with a unique account and enable multifactor authentication.", "C": "Enable timeand location-based login requirements.", "D": "Provide every staff member with a unique account and require a self-selected password."}
question_zh: Selah 希望为通过其组织的主要业务线应用程序执行的操作提供责任。在这种情况下，最常使用哪些控制措施来提供问责制？ （选择所有适用的选项。）
options_zh: {"A": "启用审核日志记录。", "B": "为每位员工提供唯一的帐户并启用多重身份验证。", "C": "启用基于时间和位置的登录要求。", "D": "为每位员工提供唯一的账户并要求自选密码。"}
explanation_en: Audit logging when combined with user accounts that can reliably be expected to only be accessible to a specific user due to the use of multifactor authentication is frequently used to provide strong accountability for actions taken via systems and applications. A password can be shared, making it less reliable, and time and location requirements are useful security controls but do not impact accountability.
explanation_zh: 审计日志记录与用户帐户结合使用时，由于使用多因素身份验证，可以可靠地预期只能由特定用户访问，因此经常用于为通过系统和应用程序采取的操作提供强有力的问责。密码可以共享，从而降低其可靠性，时间和位置要求是有用的安全控制措施，但不会影响问责制。

question_en: Charles wants to provide authorization services as part of his web application. What standard should he use if he wants to integrate easily with other web identity providers?
question_zh: Charles 希望提供授权服务作为其 Web 应用程序的一部分。如果他想轻松地与其他网络身份提供商集成，他应该使用什么标准？
options_zh: {"A": "OpenID", "B": "TACACS+", "C": "RADIUS", "D": "OAuth"}
explanation_en: OAuth is the most widely used open standard for authorization and delegation of rights for cloud services. OpenID is used for authentication, and TACAC+ and RADIUS are primarily used on-site for authentication and authorization for network devices.
explanation_zh: OAuth 是云服务授权和权限委派中使用最广泛的开放标准。 OpenID用于认证，TACAC+和RADIUS主要用于现场对网络设备进行认证和授权。

question_en: The company that Cameron works for uses a system that allows users to request privileged access to systems when necessary. Cameron requests access, and the request is pre-approved due to his role. He is then able to access the system to perform the task. Once he is done, the rights are removed. What type of system is he using?
question_zh: Cameron 工作的公司使用的系统允许用户在必要时请求对系统的特权访问。卡梅伦请求访问权限，并且由于他的角色，该请求已被预先批准。然后他就能够访问系统来执行任务。一旦他完成了，权利就被删除。他使用什么类型的系统？
options_zh: {"A": "零信任", "B": "联合身份管理", "C": "单点登录", "D": "即时访问"}
explanation_en: Cameron is using a just-in-time (JIT) system that provides the access needed when it is needed. A zero trust system requires authentication and authorization when actions are performed but does not necessarily require privileges to be granted and removed when they are needed.
explanation_zh: Cameron 使用即时 (JIT) 系统，该系统可在需要时提供所需的访问权限。零信任系统在执行操作时需要身份验证和授权，但不一定需要在需要时授予和删除特权。

question_en: Elle is responsible for building a banking website. She needs proof of the identity of the users who register for the site. How should she validate user identities?
options_en: {"A": "Require users to create unique questions that only they will know.", "B": "Require new users to bring their driver’s license or passport in person to the bank.", "C": "Use information that both the bank and the user have such as questions pulled from their credit report.", "D": "Call the user on their registered phone number to verify that they are who they claim to be."}
question_zh: Elle 负责建设一个银行网站。她需要注册该网站的用户的身份证明。她应该如何验证用户身份？
options_zh: {"A": "要求用户创建只有他们自己知道的独特问题。", "B": "要求新用户亲自携带驾照或护照前往银行。", "C": "使用银行和用户都拥有的信息，例如从他们的信用报告中提取的问题。", "D": "拨打用户注册的电话号码，以验证他们的身份。"}
explanation_en: Identity proofing can be done by comparing user information that the organization already has, like account numbers or personal information. Requiring users to create unique questions can help with future support by providing a way for them to do password resets. Using a phone call only verifies that the individual who created the account has the phone that they registered and won’t prove their identity. In-person verification would not fit the business needs of most websites.
explanation_zh: 身份证明可以通过比较组织已有的用户信息（例如帐号或个人信息）来完成。要求用户创建独特的问题可以为他们提供重置密码的方法，从而有助于获得未来的支持。使用电话只能验证创建帐户的个人是否拥有他们注册的电话，而不会证明其身份。亲自验证无法满足大多数网站的业务需求。

question_en: Susan’s organization is part of a federation that allows users from multiple organizations to access resources and services at other federated sites. When Susan wants to use a service at a partner site, which identity provider is used?
options_en: {"A": "Susan’s home organization’s identity provider", "B": "The service provider’s identity provider", "C": "Both their identity provider and the service provider’s identity provider", "D": "The service provider creates a new identity"}
question_zh: Susan 的组织是联盟的一部分，该联盟允许多个组织的用户访问其他联盟站点的资源和服务。当 Susan 想要使用合作伙伴站点的服务时，会使用哪个身份提供商？
options_zh: {"A": "Susan 的归属组织的身份提供商", "B": "服务提供商的身份提供商", "C": "他们的身份提供商和服务提供商的身份提供商", "D": "服务提供商创建新身份"}
explanation_en: Federations use a user’s home organization’s identity provider (IDP). Service providers query those identity providers when the user attempts to authenticate to the service and, if the request is validated, allow access based on the rules and policies set for the service based on attributes that may be relevant that are provided by the IDP.
explanation_zh: 联盟使用用户的归属组织的身份提供商 (IDP)。当用户尝试对服务进行身份验证时，服务提供商会查询这些身份提供商，如果请求得到验证，则根据 IDP 提供的可能相关的属性，根据为服务设置的规则和策略来允许访问。

question_en: A new customer at a bank that uses fingerprint scanners to authenticate its users is surprised when he scans his fingerprint and is logged in to another customer’s account. What type of biometric factor error occurred?
options_en: {"A": "A registration error", "B": "A Type 1 error", "C": "A Type 2 error", "D": "A time of use, method of use error"}
question_zh: 一家银行使用指纹扫描仪验证用户身份的新客户在扫描指纹并登录到另一个客户的帐户时感到惊讶。发生了什么类型的生物识别因素错误？
options_zh: {"A": "注册错误", "B": "1 类错误", "C": "2 类错误", "D": "使用时间、使用方法错误"}
explanation_en: Type 2 errors occur in biometric systems when an invalid subject is incorrectly authenticated as a valid user. In this case, nobody except the actual customer should be validated when fingerprints are scanned. Type 1 errors occur when a valid subject is not authenticated; if the existing customer was rejected, it would be a Type 1 error. Registration is the process of adding users, but registration errors and time of use, method of use errors are not specific biometric authentication terms.
explanation_zh: 当无效主体被错误地验证为有效用户时，生物识别系统中会出现 2 类错误。在这种情况下，在扫描指纹时，除了实际客户之外，任何人都不应被验证。当有效主体未经过身份验证时，会发生类型 1 错误；如果现有客户被拒绝，则属于 1 类错误。注册是添加用户的过程，但注册错误和使用时间、使用方法错误并不是具体的生物特征认证术语。

question_en: What type of access control is typically used by firewalls?
options_en: {"A": "Discretionary access controls", "B": "Rule-based access controls", "C": "Task-based access control", "D": "Mandatory access controls"}
question_zh: 防火墙通常使用什么类型的访问控制？
options_zh: {"A": "自主访问控制", "B": "基于规则的访问控制", "C": "基于任务的访问控制", "D": "强制访问控制"}
explanation_en: Firewalls use rule-based access control, or Rule-BAC, in their access control lists and apply rules created by administrators to all traffic that passes through them. DAC, or discretionary access control, allows owners to determine who can access objects they control, while task-based access control lists tasks for users. MAC, or mandatory access control, uses classifications to determine access.
explanation_zh: 防火墙在其访问控制列表中使用基于规则的访问控制（即 Rule-BAC），并将管理员创建的规则应用于通过它们的所有流量。 DAC（即自主访问控制）允许所有者确定谁可以访问他们控制的对象，而基于任务的访问控制则为用户列出任务。 MAC（强制访问控制）使用分类来确定访问。

question_en: When you input a user ID and password, you are performing what important identity and access management activity?
question_zh: 当您输入用户 ID 和密码时，您正在执行什么重要的身份和访问管理活动？
explanation_en: When you input a username and password, you are authenticating yourself by providing a unique identifier and a verification that you are the person who should have that identifier (the password). Authorization is the process of determining what a user is allowed to do. Validation and login both describe elements of what is happening in the process; however, they aren’t the most important identity and access management activity.
explanation_zh: 输入用户名和密码时，您通过提供唯一标识符并验证您是应该拥有该标识符（密码）的人来验证自己的身份。授权是确定允许用户执行哪些操作的过程。验证和登录都描述了流程中发生的事情的元素；然而，它们并不是最重要的身份和访问管理活动。

question_en: Kathleen works for a data center hosting facility that provides physical data center space for individuals and organizations. Until recently, each client was given a magnetic-strip-based keycard to access the section of the facility where their servers are located, and they were also given a key to access the cage or rack where their servers reside. In the past month, a number of servers have been stolen, but the logs for the passcards show only valid IDs. What is Kathleen’s best option to make sure that the users of the passcards are who they are supposed to be?
options_en: {"A": "Add a reader that requires a PIN for passcard users.", "B": "Add a camera system to the facility to observe who is accessing servers.", "C": "Add a biometric factor.", "D": "Replace the magnetic stripe keycards with smartcards."}
question_zh: Kathleen 在一家数据中心托管设施工作，该设施为个人和组织提供物理数据中心空间。直到最近，每个客户都获得了一张基于磁条的钥匙卡来访问其服务器所在的设施部分，并且还获得了一把钥匙来访问其服务器所在的笼子或机架。在过去的一个月里，许多服务器被盗，但密码卡的日志仅显示有效的 ID。凯瑟琳确保密码卡用户身份正确的最佳选择是什么？
options_zh: {"A": "添加需要密码卡用户 PIN 的读卡器。", "B": "在设施中添加摄像头系统，以观察谁正在访问服务器。", "C": "添加生物识别因素。", "D": "将磁条钥匙卡替换为智能卡。"}
explanation_en: Kathleen should implement a biometric factor. The cards and keys are an example of a Type 2 factor, or “something you have.” Using a smart card replaces this with another Type 2 factor, but the cards could still be loaned out or stolen. Adding a PIN suffers from the same problem: a PIN can be stolen. Adding cameras doesn’t prevent access to the facility and thus doesn’t solve the immediate problem (but it is a good idea!).
explanation_zh: 凯瑟琳应该实施生物识别因素。卡和钥匙是第 2 类因素或“你拥有的东西”的一个例子。使用智能卡可以将其替换为另一个类型 2 因素，但卡仍然可能被借出或被盗。添加 PIN 码也会遇到同样的问题：PIN 码可能会被盗。添加摄像头并不会阻止对设施的访问，因此并不能解决眼前的问题（但这是一个好主意！）。

question_en: Theresa wants to allow her staff to securely store and manage passwords for systems including service accounts and other rarely used administrative credentials. What type of tool should she implement to enable this?
options_en: {"A": "Single sign-on", "B": "A federated identity system", "C": "A password manager", "D": "A multifactor authentication system"}
question_zh: Theresa 希望允许她的员工安全地存储和管理系统密码，包括服务帐户和其他很少使用的管理凭据。她应该使用什么类型的工具来实现这一点？
options_zh: {"A": "单点登录", "B": "联合身份系统", "C": "密码管理器", "D": "多重身份验证系统"}
explanation_en: Enterprise password management tools allow passwords to be securely generated, stored, and managed. They can provide logs of who uses passwords, when they were updated, and if they meet complexity and other requirements. Of course, this means that the keys to your environment are all in one place, so securing and managing the enterprise password manager is very important!
explanation_zh: 企业密码管理工具允许安全地生成、存储和管理密码。他们可以提供有关密码使用人、密码更新时间以及密码是否满足复杂性和其他要求的日志。当然，这意味着您环境的密钥都位于一个位置，因此保护和管理企业密码管理器非常重要！

question_en: Olivia wants to limit the commands that a user can run via sudo to limit the potential for privilege escalation attacks. What Linux file should she modify to allow this?
options_en: {"A": "The bash .bin configuration file", "B": "The sudoers file", "C": "The bash .allowed configuration file", "D": "The sudont file"}
question_zh: Olivia 希望限制用户可以通过 sudo 运行的命令，以限制权限提升攻击的可能性。她应该修改哪个 Linux 文件才能实现这一点？
options_zh: {"A": "bash .bin 配置文件", "B": "sudoers 文件", "C": "bash .允许的配置文件", "D": "sudont 文件"}
explanation_en: The sudoers file can list the specific users who can use sudo as well as the commands or directories that are allowed for them.
explanation_zh: sudoers 文件可以列出可以使用 sudo 的特定用户以及允许他们使用的命令或目录。

question_en: Which objects and subjects have a label in a MAC model?
options_en: {"A": "Objects and subjects that are classified as Confidential, Secret, or Top Secret have a label.", "B": "All objects have a label, and all subjects have a compartment.", "C": "All objects and subjects have a label.", "D": "All subjects have a label and all objects have a compartment. For questions 65–67, please refer to the following scenario and diagram: Chris is the identity architect for a growing e-commerce website that wants to leverage social identity. To do this, he and his team intend to allow users to use their existing Google accounts as their primary accounts when using the e-commerce site. This means that when a new user initially connects to the e-commerce platform, they are given the choice between using their Google account using OAuth 2.0 or creating a new account on the platform using their own email address and a password of their choice. E-commerce Google Servers Application Servers 1. Anti-forgery token created 2. User login and consent to data release 3. Token confirmed and authorization code sent Client 4. Exchange code for access and ID token 5. Token response 6. Call Google API using token"}
question_zh: MAC 模型中哪些对象和主体有标签？
options_zh: {"A": "被分类为机密、秘密或绝密的对象和主题有一个标签。", "B": "所有物体都有标签，所有主体都有隔间。", "C": "所有物体和主体都有一个标签。", "D": "所有主体都有标签，所有物体都有隔间。对于问题 65-67，请参考以下场景和图表： Chris 是一家不断发展的电子商务网站的身份架构师，该网站希望利用社交身份。为此，他和他的团队打算允许用户在使用电子商务网站时使用现有的 Google 帐户作为主要帐户。这意味着，当新用户最初连接到电子商务平台时，他们可以选择是使用 OAuth 2.0 使用 Google 帐户，还是使用自己的电子邮件地址和自己选择的密码在平台上创建新帐户。电子商务 Google 服务器 应用服务器 1. 创建防伪令牌 2. 用户登录并同意数据发布 3. 令牌确认并发送授权代码 客户端 4. 交换访问代码和 ID 令牌 5. 令牌响应 6. 使用令牌调用 Google API"}
explanation_en: In a mandatory access control system, all subjects and objects have a label. Compartments may or may not be used, but there is not a specific requirement for either subjects or objects to be compartmentalized. The specific labels of Confidential, Secret, and Top Secret are not required by MAC.
explanation_zh: 在强制访问控制系统中，所有主体和客体都有标签。可以使用也可以不使用隔室，但是对于要划分的主体或对象没有具体要求。 MAC 不要求 Confidential、Secret 和 Top Secret 的具体标签。

question_en: When the e-commerce application creates an account for a Google user, where should that user’s password be stored?
options_en: {"A": "The password is stored in the e-commerce application’s database.", "B": "The password is stored in memory on the e-commerce application’s server.", "C": "The password is stored in Google’s account management system.", "D": "The password is never stored; instead, a salted hash is stored in Google’s account management system."}
question_zh: 当电子商务应用程序为 Google 用户创建帐户时，该用户的密码应该存储在哪里？
options_zh: {"A": "密码存储在电子商务应用程序的数据库中。", "B": "密码存储在电子商务应用程序服务器的内存中。", "C": "密码存储在 Google 的帐户管理系统中。", "D": "密码永远不会被存储；相反，加盐哈希值存储在 Google 的帐户管理系统中。"}
explanation_en: Passwords are never stored for web applications in a well-designed environment. Instead, salted hashes are stored and compared to passwords after they are salted and hashed. If the hashes match, the user is authenticated.
explanation_zh: 在设计良好的环境中，永远不会为 Web 应用程序存储密码。相反，加盐哈希值会被存储，并在加盐和哈希处理后与密码进行比较。如果哈希值匹配，则用户通过身份验证。

question_en: Which of the following is responsible for user authentication for Google users?
options_en: {"A": "The e-commerce application.", "B": "Both the e-commerce application and Google servers.", "C": "Google servers.", "D": "The diagram does not provide enough information to determine this."}
question_zh: 以下哪项负责 Google 用户的用户身份验证？
options_zh: {"A": "电子商务应用程序。", "B": "电子商务应用程序和 Google 服务器。", "C": "谷歌服务器。", "D": "该图没有提供足够的信息来确定这一点。"}
explanation_en: When a third-party site integrates via OAuth 2.0, authentication is handled by the service provider’s servers. In this case, Google is acting as the service provider for user authentication. Authentication for local users who create their own accounts would occur in the e-commerce application (or a related server), but that is not the question that is asked here.
explanation_zh: 当第三方站点通过 OAuth 2.0 集成时，身份验证由服务提供商的服务器处理。在这种情况下，Google 充当用户身份验证的服务提供商。创建自己帐户的本地用户的身份验证将在电子商务应用程序（或相关服务器）中进行，但这不是此处提出的问题。

question_en: What type of attack is the creation and exchange of state tokens intended to prevent?
question_zh: 状态代币的创建和交换旨在防止什么类型的攻击？
options_zh: {"A": "XSS", "B": "CSRF", "C": "SQL 注入", "D": "XACML"}
explanation_en: The anti-forgery state token exchanged during OAuth sessions is intended to prevent cross-site request forgery. This makes sure that the unique session token with the authentication response from Google’s OAuth service is available to verify that the user, not an attacker, is making a request. XSS attacks focus on scripting and would have script tags involved, SQL injection would have SQL code included, and XACML is the eXtensible Access Control Markup Language, not a type of attack.
explanation_zh: OAuth 会话期间交换的防伪造状态令牌旨在防止跨站点请求伪造。这确保了带有来自 Google OAuth 服务的身份验证响应的唯一会话令牌可用于验证用户（而不是攻击者）正在发出请求。 XSS攻击侧重于脚本编写，会涉及脚本标签，SQL注入会包含SQL代码，而XACML是可扩展访问控制标记语言，而不是一种攻击。

question_en: Questions like “What is your pet’s name?” are examples of what type of identity proofing?
options_en: {"A": "Knowledge-based authentication", "B": "Dynamic knowledge-based authentication", "C": "Out-of-band identity proofing", "D": "A Type 3 authentication factor"}
question_zh: “你的宠物叫什么名字？”之类的问题举例说明了哪些类型的身份证明？
options_zh: {"A": "基于知识的身份验证", "B": "动态基于知识的身份验证", "C": "带外身份证明", "D": "类型 3 身份验证因素"}
explanation_en: Knowledge-based authentication relies on preset questions such as “What is your pet’s name?” and the answers. It can be susceptible to attacks because of the availability of the answers on social media or other sites. Dynamic knowledge-based authentication relies on facts or data that the user already knows that can be used to create questions they can answer on an as-needed basis (for example, a previous address or a school they attended). Out-of-band identity proofing relies on an alternate channel like a phone call or text message. Finally, Type 3 authentication factors are biometric, or “something you are,” rather than knowledge-based.
explanation_zh: 基于知识的身份验证依赖于预设问题，例如“您的宠物叫什么名字？”以及答案。由于可以在社交媒体或其他网站上找到答案，因此它可能容易受到攻击。基于知识的动态身份验证依赖于用户已知的事实或数据，这些事实或数据可用于创建他们可以根据需要回答的问题（例如，以前的地址或他们就读的学校）。带外身份验证依赖于备用渠道，例如电话或短信。最后，第 3 类身份验证因素是生物识别因素，或“你是谁”，而不是基于知识的因素。

question_en: Madhuri creates a table that includes assigned privileges, objects, and subjects to manage access control for the systems she is responsible for. Each time a subject attempts to access an object, the systems check the table to ensure that the subject has the appropriate rights to the objects. What type of access control system is Madhuri using?
options_en: {"A": "A capability table", "B": "An access control list", "C": "An access control matrix", "D": "A subject/object rights management system"}
question_zh: Madhuri 创建一个表，其中包含分配的权限、对象和主题，以管理她负责的系统的访问控制。每次主体尝试访问客体时，系统都会检查该表以确保主体对客体具有适当的权限。 Madhuri 使用什么类型的访问控制系统？
options_zh: {"A": "能力表", "B": "访问控制列表", "C": "访问控制矩阵", "D": "主体/客体权限管理系统"}
explanation_en: An access control matrix is a table that lists objects, subjects, and their privileges. Access control lists focus on objects and which subjects can access them. Capability tables list subjects and what objects they can access. Subject/object rights management systems are not based on an access control model.
explanation_zh: 访问控制矩阵是列出客体、主体及其权限的表。访问控制列表重点关注对象以及哪些主体可以访问它们。能力表列出了主题以及他们可以访问的对象。主体/客体权限管理系统不基于访问控制模型。

question_en: During a review of support tickets, Ben’s organization discovered that password changes accounted for more than a quarter of its help desk’s cases. Which of the following options would be most likely to decrease that number significantly?
question_zh: 在审查支持票证时，Ben 的组织发现密码更改占其服务台案例的四分之一以上。以下哪个选项最有可能显着减少该数字？
options_zh: {"A": "双因素身份验证", "B": "生物识别身份验证", "C": "自助密码重置", "D": "密码短语"}
explanation_en: Self-service password reset tools typically have a significant impact on the number of password reset contacts that a help desk has. Two-factor and biometric authentication both add complexity and may actually increase the number of contacts. Passphrases can be easier to remember than traditional complex passwords and may decrease calls, but they don’t have the same impact that a self-service system does.
explanation_zh: 自助密码重置工具通常会对帮助台拥有的密码重置联系人数量产生重大影响。双因素身份验证和生物识别身份验证都会增加复杂性，并且实际上可能会增加联系人的数量。密码短语比传统的复杂密码更容易记住，并且可能会减少呼叫次数，但它们不会产生与自助服务系统相同的影响。

question_en: Brian’s large organization has used RADIUS for AAA services for its network devices for years and has recently become aware of security issues with the unencrypted information transferred during authentication. How should Brian implement encryption for RADIUS?
options_en: {"A": "Use the built-in encryption in RADIUS.", "B": "Implement RADIUS over its native UDP using TLS for protection.", "C": "Implement RADIUS over TCP using TLS for protection.", "D": "Use an AES256 pre-shared cipher between devices."}
question_zh: Brian 的大型组织多年来一直在其网络设备上使用 RADIUS 进行 AAA 服务，并且最近意识到身份验证过程中传输的未加密信息存在安全问题。 Brian 应如何为 RADIUS 实施加密？
options_zh: {"A": "使用 RADIUS 中的内置加密。", "B": "使用 TLS 通过其本机 UDP 实施 RADIUS 进行保护。", "C": "使用 TLS 实现基于 TCP 的 RADIUS 进行保护。", "D": "在设备之间使用 AES256 预共享密码。"}
explanation_en: RADIUS supports TLS over TCP. RADIUS does not have a supported TLS mode over UDP. AES pre-shared symmetric ciphers are not a supported solution and would be difficult to both implement and maintain in a large environment, and the built-in encryption in RADIUS only protects passwords.
explanation_zh: RADIUS 支持基于 TCP 的 TLS。 RADIUS 不支持基于 UDP 的 TLS 模式。 AES 预共享对称密码不是受支持的解决方案，并且难以在大型环境中实施和维护，并且 RADIUS 中的内置加密仅保护密码。

question_en: Jim wants to allow cloud-based applications to act on his behalf to access information from other sites. Which of the following tools can allow that?
question_zh: Jim 希望允许基于云的应用程序代表他访问其他站点的信息。以下哪些工具可以实现这一点？
options_zh: {"A": "Kerberos", "B": "OAuth", "C": "OpenID", "D": "LDAP"}
explanation_en: OAuth provides the ability to access resources from another service and would meet Jim’s needs. OpenID would allow him to use an account from another service with his application, and Kerberos and LDAP are used more frequently for in-house services.
explanation_zh: OAuth 提供了从其他服务访问资源的能力，并且可以满足 Jim 的需求。 OpenID 允许他在他的应用程序中使用来自其他服务的帐户，而 Kerberos 和 LDAP 更频繁地用于内部服务。

question_en: Ben’s organization has had an issue with unauthorized access to applications and workstations during the lunch hour when employees aren’t at their desk. What are the best types of session management solutions for Ben to recommend to help prevent this type of access?
options_en: {"A": "Use session IDs for all access and verify system IP addresses of all workstations.", "B": "Set session timeouts for applications and use password-protected screensavers with inactivity timeouts on workstations.", "C": "Use session IDs for all applications, and use password-protected screensavers with inactivity timeouts on workstations.", "D": "Set session timeouts for applications and verify system IP addresses of all workstations."}
question_zh: Ben 的组织遇到了在午餐时间员工不在办公桌前未经授权访问应用程序和工作站的问题。 Ben 推荐哪些最佳类型的会话管理解决方案来帮助防止此类访问？
options_zh: {"A": "对所有访问使用会话 ID，并验证所有工作站的系统 IP 地址。", "B": "设置应用程序的会话超时，并在工作站上使用受密码保护的屏幕保护程序和不活动超时。", "C": "对所有应用程序使用会话 ID，并在工作站上使用具有不活动超时功能的受密码保护的屏幕保护程序。", "D": "设置应用程序的会话超时并验证所有工作站的系统 IP 地址。"}
explanation_en: Since physical access to the workstations is part of the problem, setting application timeouts and password-protected screensavers with relatively short inactivity timeouts can help prevent unauthorized access. Using session IDs for all applications and verifying system IP addresses would be helpful for online attacks against applications.
explanation_zh: 由于对工作站的物理访问是问题的一部分，因此设置应用程序超时和密码保护的屏幕保护程序以及相对较短的不活动超时可以帮助防止未经授权的访问。对所有应用程序使用会话 ID 并验证系统 IP 地址将有助于针对应用程序的在线攻击。

question_en: What type of authentication scenario is shown in the following diagram? Onpremise datacenter Authentication Trust relationship Cloud Service access service provider
question_zh: 下图所示是什么类型的身份验证场景？本地数据中心 身份验证 信任关系 云服务访问服务提供商
options_zh: {"A": "混合联合", "B": "本地联合", "C": "云联合", "D": "Kerberos 联合"}
explanation_en: This diagram shows an example of hybrid federation where authentication occurs onpremises and services are provided through a federated identity service in the cloud.
explanation_zh: 此图显示了混合联合示例，其中身份验证在本地进行，服务通过云中的联合身份服务提供。

question_en: Chris wants to control access to his facility while still identifying individuals. He also wants to ensure that the individuals are the people who are being admitted without significant ongoing costs. Which solutions from the following options would meet all of these requirements? (Select all that apply.)
options_en: {"A": "Security guards and photo identification badges", "B": "RFID badges and readers with PIN pads", "C": "Magstripe badges and readers with PIN pads", "D": "Security guards and magstripe readers"}
question_zh: Chris 希望控制对其设施的访问，同时仍能识别个人身份。他还希望确保这些人是在没有重大持续费用的情况下被接纳的人。以下选项中的哪些解决方案可以满足所有这些要求？ （选择所有适用项。）
options_zh: {"A": "保安人员和照片识别徽章", "B": "RFID 徽章和带密码键盘的读卡器", "C": "磁条徽章和带密码键盘的读卡器", "D": "保安人员和磁条读卡器"}
explanation_en: The best answers in the scenario that Chris faces are either RFID or magstripe readers and PIN pads. Guards create ongoing expenses, and any solution without a PIN will allow a stolen or cloned badge to be used without validating that the person accessing the building is a legitimate user. While a guard can prevent a stolen badge and PIN combination, this is only used in environments where the cost is justifiable.
explanation_zh: Chris 面临的场景中的最佳答案是 RFID 或磁条读卡器和密码键盘。警卫会产生持续的费用，任何没有 PIN 的解决方案都将允许使用被盗或克隆的徽章，而无需验证进入建筑物的人是否是合法用户。虽然警卫可以防止徽章和 PIN 组合被盗，但这仅在成本合理的环境中使用。

question_en: A device like Yubikey or Titan Security Key is what type of Type 2 authentication factor?
options_en: {"A": "A token", "B": "A biometric identifier", "C": "A smart card", "D": "A PIV"}
question_zh: Yubikey 或 Titan 安全密钥等设备是哪种类型的 2 类身份验证因素？
options_zh: {"A": "令牌", "B": "生物识别标识符", "C": "智能卡", "D": "PIV"}
explanation_en: Yubikeys, Titan Security Keys, and similar devices are examples of tokens. PIV stands for personal identity verification and is a full multifactor authentication solution, not a device. Biometric identifiers are something you are, and a smart card is a card with an embedded chip.
explanation_zh: Yubikey、Titan 安全密钥和类似设备都是令牌的示例。 PIV 代表个人身份验证，是一个完整的多因素身份验证解决方案，而不是设备。生物识别标识符是你的本质，而智能卡是带有嵌入式芯片的卡。

question_en: What authentication technology can be paired with OAuth to perform identity verification and obtain user profile information using a RESTful API?
question_zh: 什么认证技术可以与OAuth配合使用RESTful API进行身份验证并获取用户个人资料信息？
options_zh: {"A": "SAML", "B": "Shibboleth", "C": "OpenID Connect", "D": "Higgins"}
explanation_en: OpenID Connect is a RESTful, JSON-based authentication protocol that, when paired with OAuth, can provide identity verification and basic profile information. SAML is the Security Assertion Markup Language, Shibboleth is a federated identity solution designed to allow web-based SSO, and Higgins is an open source project designed to provide users with control over the release of their identity information.
explanation_zh: OpenID Connect 是一种基于 JSON 的 RESTful 身份验证协议，与 OAuth 配合使用时，可以提供身份验证和基本配置文件信息。 SAML 是安全断言标记语言，Shibboleth 是一种联合身份解决方案，旨在允许基于 Web 的 SSO，Higgins 是一个开源项目，旨在为用户提供对其身份信息发布的控制。

question_en: Jim wants to implement an access control scheme that will ensure that users cannot delegate access. He also wants to enforce access control at the operating system level. What access control mechanism best fits these requirements?
options_en: {"A": "Role-based access control", "B": "Discretionary access control", "C": "Mandatory access control", "D": "Attribute-based access control"}
question_zh: Jim 希望实施一种访问控制方案，以确保用户无法委派访问权限。他还希望在操作系统级别实施访问控制。哪种访问控制机制最适合这些要求？
options_zh: {"A": "基于角色的访问控制", "B": "自主访问控制", "C": "强制访问控制", "D": "基于属性的访问控制"}
explanation_en: In a mandatory access control system, the operating system enforces access control, and users cannot delegate rights. Discretionary access control allows users to delegate rights, and neither attribute nor role-based access control specifically meets these requirements.
explanation_zh: 在强制访问控制系统中，操作系统强制执行访问控制，用户不能委派权限。自主访问控制允许用户委派权限，并且基于属性和基于角色的访问控制都不能专门满足这些要求。

question_en: The security administrators at the company that Susan works for have configured the workstation she uses to allow her to log in only during her work hours. What type of access control best describes this limitation?
question_zh: Susan 所在公司的安全管理员将她使用的工作站配置为仅允许她在工作时间登录。哪种类型的访问控制最能描述这种限制？
options_zh: {"A": "约束接口", "B": "上下文相关控制", "C": "内容相关控制", "D": "最低权限"}
explanation_en: Time-based controls are an example of context-dependent controls. A constrained interface would limit what Susan was able to do in an application or system interface, while content-dependent control would limit her access to content based on her role or rights. Least privilege is used to ensure that subjects only receive the rights they need to perform their role.
explanation_zh: 基于时间的控件是上下文相关控件的示例。受约束的界面将限制 Susan 在应用程序或系统界面中能够执行的操作，而内容相关的控制将根据她的角色或权限限制她对内容的访问。最小特权用于确保主体仅获得履行其角色所需的权限。

question_en: Ben uses a software-based token that changes its code every minute. What type of token is he using?
question_zh: Ben 使用基于软件的令牌，每分钟都会更改其代码。他使用什么类型的令牌？
explanation_en: Synchronous soft tokens, such as Google Authenticator, use a time-based algorithm that generates a constantly changing series of codes. Asynchronous tokens typically require a challenge to be entered on the token to allow it to calculate a response, which the server compares to the response it expects. Smartcards typically present a certificate but may have other token capabilities built-in. Static tokens are physical devices that can contain credentials and include smart cards and memory cards.
explanation_zh: 同步软令牌（例如 Google Authenticator）使用基于时间的算法，生成一系列不断变化的代码。异步令牌通常需要在令牌上输入质询，以允许其计算响应，服务器将其与它期望的响应进行比较。智能卡通常提供证书，但也可能内置其他令牌功能。静态令牌是可以包含凭据的物理设备，包括智能卡和存储卡。

question_en: Firewalls are an example of what type of access control mechanism?
options_en: {"A": "Mandatory access control", "B": "Attribute-based access control", "C": "Discretionary access control", "D": "Rule-based access control"}
question_zh: 防火墙是哪种类型的访问控制机制的示例？
options_zh: {"A": "强制访问控制", "B": "基于属性的访问控制", "C": "自主访问控制", "D": "基于规则的访问控制"}
explanation_en: Firewalls operate based on a ruleset and are an example of a rule-based access control scheme.
explanation_zh: 防火墙基于规则集运行，是基于规则的访问控制方案的示例。

question_en: Michelle works for a financial services company and wants to register customers for her web application. What type of authentication mechanism could she use for the initial login if she wants to quickly and automatically verify that the person is who they claim to be without having a previous relationship with them?
options_en: {"A": "Request their Social Security number.", "B": "Use knowledge-based authentication.", "C": "Perform manual identity verification.", "D": "Use a biometric factor."}
question_zh: Michelle 在一家金融服务公司工作，想要为她的 Web 应用程序注册客户。如果她想快速、自动地验证该人是否是他们声称的人，而无需与他们之前有任何关系，那么她可以在初始登录中使用什么类型的身份验证机制？
options_zh: {"A": "索取他们的社会安全号码。", "B": "使用基于知识的身份验证。", "C": "进行手动身份验证。", "D": "使用生物识别因素。"}
explanation_en: Knowledge-based authentication is used by some financial institutions to validate the identity of new users. It uses information from tax and financial records that is unlikely to be available to others, allowing new users to provide details like their last credit card payment, mortgage payment, or other information to validate their identity. A Social Security number is somewhat trivial to acquire via paid services or other means, and manually validating identities is neither quick nor automatic. A biometric factor would require a previous enrollment, making this unsuitable for new customers.
explanation_zh: 一些金融机构使用基于知识的身份验证来验证新用户的身份。它使用其他人不太可能获得的税务和财务记录信息，允许新用户提供上次信用卡付款、抵押贷款付款或其他信息等详细信息来验证其身份。通过付费服务或其他方式获取社会安全号码有些微不足道，并且手动验证身份既不快速也不自动。生物识别因素需要事先注册，因此不适合新客户。

question_en: Megan’s company wants to use Google accounts to allow users to quickly adopt their web application. What common cloud federation technologies will Megan need to implement? (Select all that apply.)
question_zh: Megan 的公司希望使用 Google 帐户让用户快速采用他们的 Web 应用程序。 Megan 需要实施哪些常见的云联合技术？ （选择所有适用项。）
options_zh: {"A": "Kerberos", "B": "OpenID", "C": "OAuth", "D": "RADIUS"}
explanation_en: Google accounts like many cloud identity providers rely on OpenID and OAuth. Kerberos is used for on-premises environments, and RADIUS is frequently used for authentication and authorization for network devices and services like VPN.
explanation_zh: Google 帐户与许多云身份提供商一样依赖 OpenID 和 OAuth。 Kerberos 用于本地环境，RADIUS 经常用于网络设备和服务（例如 VPN）的身份验证和授权。

question_en: Session ID length and session ID entropy are both important to prevent what type of attack?
question_zh: 会话ID长度和会话ID熵对于防止什么类型的攻击都很重要？
options_zh: {"A": "拒绝服务", "B": "Cookie 盗窃", "C": "会话猜测", "D": "中间人攻击"}
explanation_en: Best practices for session management involve a long session ID (often 128 bits or longer) and enough randomness or entropy to make it hard to guess session IDs. This makes brute-force or algorithmic guessing attacks unlikely unless there is a flaw in the implementation. These do not prevent denial-of-service or man-in-the-middle attacks, and cookie attacks are focused on acquiring and reading or reusing cookies in most scenarios.
explanation_zh: 会话管理的最佳实践涉及长会话 ID（通常为 128 位或更长）和足够的随机性或熵，以使其难以猜测会话 ID。这使得暴力或算法猜测攻击不太可能发生，除非实现中存在缺陷。这些并不能防止拒绝服务或中间人攻击，并且 cookie 攻击在大多数情况下集中于获取和读取或重用 cookie。

question_en: The access control system for Naomi’s organization checks if her computer is fully patched, if it has a successful clean anti-malware scan, and if the firewall is turned on among other security validations before it allows her to connect to the network. If there are potential issues, she is not permitted to connect and must contact support. What type of access control scheme best describes this type of process?
options_en: {"A": "MAC", "B": "Rule-based access control", "C": "Role-based access control", "D": "Risk-based access control"}
question_zh: Naomi 组织的访问控制系统会检查她的计算机是否已完全修补、是否成功进行了干净的反恶意软件扫描以及防火墙是否已打开以及其他安全验证，然后才允许她连接到网络。如果存在潜在问题，她将不允许连接，必须联系支持人员。哪种类型的访问控制方案最能描述此类过程？
options_zh: {"A": "MAC", "B": "基于规则的访问控制", "C": "基于角色的访问控制", "D": "基于风险的访问控制"}
explanation_en: Risk-based access control models risk using information that is available when the access request is created. Information about the request and the risk it may create is calculated based on risk values and compared to access policies. If the risk value is acceptable, access is granted. One of the most common examples of this in organizations is NAC, or network access control, where a system is profiled to determine security risk and compliance before admission to a network. This can be seen as a more specific example of rule-based access control. Role-based access control bases its decisions on the roles of the individuals, whereas mandatory access control is enforced by the operating system.
explanation_zh: 基于风险的访问控制模型存在使用创建访问请求时可用的信息的风险。有关请求及其可能创建的风险的信息是根据风险值计算的，并与访问策略进行比较。如果风险值可接受，则授予访问权限。组织中最常见的示例之一是 NAC（网络访问控制），其中对系统进行分析以确定系统在进入网络之前确定安全风险和合规性。这可以看作是基于规则的访问控制的更具体的示例。基于角色的访问控制根据个人的角色做出决策，而强制访问控制则由操作系统强制执行。

question_en: Isabelle wants to prevent privilege escalation attacks via her organization’s service accounts. Which of the following security practices is best suited to this?
options_en: {"A": "Remove unnecessary rights.", "B": "Disable interactive login for service accounts.", "C": "Limit when accounts can log in.", "D": "Use meaningless or randomized names for service accounts."}
question_zh: Isabelle 希望通过其组织的服务帐户防止权限升级攻击。以下哪种安全实践最适合这种情况？
options_zh: {"A": "删除不必要的权利。", "B": "禁用服务帐户的交互式登录。", "C": "限制帐户可以登录的时间。", "D": "对服务帐户使用无意义或随机的名称。"}
explanation_en: The most important step in securing service accounts is to ensure that they have only the rights that are absolutely needed to accomplish the task they are designed for. Disabling interactive logins is important as well and would be the next best answer. Limiting when accounts can log in and using randomized or meaningless account names can both be helpful in some circumstances but are far less important.
explanation_zh: 确保服务帐户安全的最重要步骤是确保它们仅拥有完成其设计任务所绝对需要的权限。禁用交互式登录也很重要，并且将是下一个最佳答案。在某些情况下，限制帐户可以登录的时间以及使用随机或无意义的帐户名都可能有所帮助，但远没有那么重要。

question_en: What danger is created by allowing the OpenID relying party to control the connection to the OpenID provider?
options_en: {"A": "It may cause incorrect selection of the proper OpenID provider.", "B": "It creates the possibility of a phishing attack by sending data to a fake OpenID provider.", "C": "The relying party may be able to steal the client’s username and password.", "D": "The relying party may not send a signed assertion."}
question_zh: 允许 OpenID 依赖方控制与 OpenID 提供者的连接会产生什么危险？
options_zh: {"A": "可能会导致正确选择 OpenID 提供商的错误。", "B": "它通过向虚假 OpenID 提供商发送数据而造成网络钓鱼攻击的可能性。", "C": "依赖方可能能够窃取客户端的用户名和密码。", "D": "依赖方不得发送签名断言。"}
explanation_en: Allowing the relying party to provide the redirect to the OpenID provider could allow a phishing attack by directing clients to a fake OpenID provider that can capture valid credentials. Since the OpenID provider URL is provided by the client, the relying party cannot select the wrong provider. The relying party never receives the user’s password, which means that they can’t steal it. Finally, the relying party receives the signed assertion but does not send one.
explanation_zh: 允许依赖方提供到 OpenID 提供商的重定向可能会通过将客户端定向到可以捕获有效凭据的虚假 OpenID 提供商来允许网络钓鱼攻击。由于 OpenID 提供商 URL 由客户端提供，依赖方无法选择错误的提供商。依赖方永远不会收到用户的密码，这意味着他们无法窃取它。最后，依赖方收到签名的断言，但不发送。

question_en: Jim is implementing a cloud identity solution for his organization. What type of technology is he putting in place?
options_en: {"A": "Identity as a service", "B": "Employee ID as a service", "C": "Cloud-based RADIUS", "D": "OAuth"}
question_zh: Jim 正在为其组织实施云身份解决方案。他正在采用什么类型的技术？
options_zh: {"A": "身份即服务", "B": "员工 ID 即服务", "C": "基于云的 RADIUS", "D": "OAuth"}
explanation_en: IDaaS, or identity as a service, provides an identity platform as a third-party service. This can provide benefits including integration with cloud services and removing overhead for maintenance of traditional on-premises identity systems, but it can also create risk due to third-party control of identity services and reliance on an off-site identity infrastructure.
explanation_zh: IDaaS，或身份即服务，提供身份平台作为第三方服务。这可以带来好处，包括与云服务集成以及消除传统本地身份系统的维护开销，但也可能由于身份服务的第三方控制和对异地身份基础设施的依赖而产生风险。

question_en: Kristen wants to control access to an application in her organization based on a combination of staff member’s job titles, the permissions each group of titles need for the application, and the time of day and location. What type of control scheme should she select?
question_zh: Kristen 希望根据员工的职务、每组职务所需的权限以及一天中的时间和位置来控制对组织中应用程序的访问。她应该选择什么类型的控制方案？
options_zh: {"A": "ABAC", "B": "DAC", "C": "MAC", "D": "角色 BAC"}
explanation_en: Attributes used for ABAC often fall into one of four categories: subject attributes like department or title; action attributes like the ability to view, edit, or delete; object attributes that describe the object that can be impacted; and contextual attributes like location, time, or elements. Discretionary access control would place these decisions in the hands of trusted subjects, MAC would enforce it at the operating system level, and role BAC would use only roles instead of the full set of criteria Kristen wants to apply.
explanation_zh: 用于 ABAC 的属性通常属于以下四类之一： 主题属性，如部门或职称；操作属性，例如查看、编辑或删除的能力；描述可能受影响的对象的对象属性；以及位置、时间或元素等上下文属性。自主访问控制将这些决策交给受信任的主体，MAC 将在操作系统级别强制执行，角色 BAC 将仅使用角色，而不是 Kristen 想要应用的全套标准。

question_en: When Alex sets the permissions shown in the following image as one of many users on a Linux server, what type of access control model is he leveraging?
options_en: {"A": "Role-based access control", "B": "Rule-based access control", "C": "Mandatory access control (MAC)", "D": "Discretionary access control (DAC)"}
question_zh: 当 Alex 将下图所示的权限设置为 Linux 服务器上的众多用户之一时，他利用的是哪种类型的访问控制模型？
options_zh: {"A": "基于角色的访问控制", "B": "基于规则的访问控制", "C": "强制访问控制 (MAC)", "D": "自主访问控制 (DAC)"}
explanation_en: The Linux filesystem allows the owners of objects to determine the access rights that subjects have to them. This means that it is a discretionary access control. If the system enforced a role-based access control, Alex wouldn’t set the controls; they would be set based on the roles assigned to each subject. A rule-based access control system would apply rules throughout the system, and a mandatory access control system uses classification labels.
explanation_zh: Linux 文件系统允许对象的所有者确定主体对其拥有的访问权限。这意味着它是一种自主访问控制。如果系统强制执行基于角色的访问控制，Alex 就不会设置控制；它们将根据分配给每个主题的角色来设置。基于规则的访问控制系统将在整个系统中应用规则，而强制访问控制系统则使用分类标签。

question_en: Joanna leads her organization’s identity management team and wants to ensure that roles are properly updated when staff members change to new positions. What issue should she focus on for those staff members to avoid future issues with role definition?
question_zh: Joanna 领导其组织的身份管理团队，并希望确保当员工调任新职位时正确更新角色。她应该为这些员工关注哪些问题，以避免将来出现角色定义问题？
explanation_en: Privilege creep is a constant concern when staff change roles over time. Privileges from previous roles may be easy to forget or to retain during transition because staff may continue to help cover the tasks or processes the individual previously performed. Over time, these forgotten rights and privileges can stack, leaving the staff member with rights that their current role should not have. Registration is a concern for new staff, while de-provisioning is a concern for departing staff. Accountability is typically provided by IAM systems by authenticating and logging access and privilege usage.
explanation_zh: 当员工随着时间的推移改变角色时，特权蔓延是一个持续存在的问题。在过渡期间，以前角色的特权可能很容易忘记或保留，因为员工可能会继续帮助完成个人以前执行的任务或流程。随着时间的推移，这些被遗忘的权利和特权可能会叠加，使员工拥有其当前角色不应拥有的权利。注册是新员工关心的问题，而取消配置是离职员工关心的问题。问责制通常由 IAM 系统通过验证和记录访问和权限使用来提供。

question_en: What type of authorization mechanism is shown in the following chart? Group Privileges System administrators Superuser on a desktop, domain administrator Application administrators Sudo privileges on application servers Database administrators Sudo privileges on database servers Users User rights on desktop workstations
question_zh: 下图所示的授权机制是什么类型？组权限 系统管理员 桌面上的超级用户、域管理员 应用程序管理员 应用程序服务器上的 Sudo 权限 数据库管理员 数据库服务器上的 Sudo 权限 用户 桌面工作站上的用户权限
options_zh: {"A": "RBAC", "B": "ABAC", "C": "MAC", "D": "DAC"}
explanation_en: This is a role-based access control (RBAC) chart noting that each group has specific rights by roles. Attribute-based access control (ABAC) would use other attributes including things like location, mandatory access control (MAC) would be enforced by the operating system, and discretionary access control (DAC) allows subjects like users to set rights on objects they control.
explanation_zh: 这是基于角色的访问控制 (RBAC) 图表，指出每个组都按角色拥有特定权限。基于属性的访问控制 (ABAC) 将使用其他属性，包括位置等属性，强制访问控制 (MAC) 将由操作系统强制执行，而自主访问控制 (DAC) 允许用户等主体对其控制的对象设置权限。

question_en: Susan is troubleshooting Kerberos authentication problems with symptoms including TGTs that are not accepted as valid and an inability to receive new tickets. If the system she is troubleshooting is properly configured for Kerberos authentication, her username and password are correct, and her network connection is functioning, what is the most likely issue?
options_en: {"A": "The Kerberos server is offline.", "B": "There is a protocol mismatch.", "C": "The client’s TGTs have been marked as compromised and de-authorized.", "D": "The Kerberos server and the local client’s time clocks are not synchronized."}
question_zh: Susan 正在对 Kerberos 身份验证问题进行故障排除，其症状包括 TGT 不被接受为有效以及无法接收新票证。如果她正在排除故障的系统已正确配置 Kerberos 身份验证，她的用户名和密码正确，并且网络连接正常运行，那么最可能的问题是什么？
options_zh: {"A": "Kerberos 服务器离线。", "B": "协议不匹配。", "C": "客户的 TGT 已被标记为受损并被取消授权。", "D": "Kerberos 服务器和本地客户端的时钟不同步。"}
explanation_en: Kerberos relies on properly synchronized time on each end of a connection to function. If the local system time is more than five minutes out of sync, valid TGTs will be invalid, and the system won’t receive any new tickets.
explanation_zh: Kerberos 依赖于连接两端正确同步的时间来发挥作用。如果本地系统时间不同步超过五分钟，有效的 TGT 将失效，并且系统不会收到任何新的票据。

question_en: Brian wants to explain the benefits of an on-premise federation approach for identity to his organization’s leadership. Which of the following is not a common benefit of a federated identity system?
options_en: {"A": "Ease of account management", "B": "Single sign-on", "C": "Prevention of brute-force attacks", "D": "Increased productivity"}
question_zh: Brian 想向其组织领导层解释本地联合身份验证方法的好处。以下哪一项不是联合身份系统的常见好处？
options_zh: {"A": "简化帐户管理", "B": "单点登录", "C": "防止暴力攻击", "D": "提高工作效率"}
explanation_en: Single sign-on (SSO) is part of identity federation. It also means that account management is simpler since multiple accounts don’t have to be maintained for users who need to access systems and resources across the federation. Productivity can increase because staff don’t have to remember multiple logins and can use SSO to log in once instead of multiple times. It does not, however, do anything to prevent brute-force attacks, and in fact, a single account with broad access can make it easier for an attacker to gain that broader access unless solutions like multifactor authentication are put in place.
explanation_zh: 单点登录 (SSO) 是身份联合的一部分。这还意味着帐户管理更加简单，因为无需为需要访问整个联盟的系统和资源的用户维护多个帐户。工作效率可以提高，因为员工不必记住多次登录，并且可以使用 SSO 登录一次而不是多次。然而，它并没有采取任何措施来防止暴力攻击，事实上，具有广泛访问权限的单个帐户可以使攻击者更容易获得更广泛的访问权限，除非采取多因素身份验证等解决方案。

question_en: The bank that Aaron works for wants to allow customers to use a new add-on application from a third-party partner they are working with. Since not every customer will want or need an account, Aaron has suggested that the bank use a SAML-based workflow that creates an account when a user downloads the app and tries to log in. What type of provisioning system has he suggested?
question_zh: Aaron 工作的银行希望允许客户使用与他们合作的第三方合作伙伴提供的新附加应用程序。由于并非每个客户都想要或需要帐户，Aaron 建议银行使用基于 SAML 的工作流程，在用户下载应用程序并尝试登录时创建帐户。他建议使用哪种类型的配置系统？
